question.text: What is the minimum asset value requirement for a REIT coming out with an initial offer?

answer.text: Rs. 500 crore with minimum offer size of Rs. 250 crore
Rs. 250 crore with minimum offer size of Rs. 100 crore
Rs. 1,000 crore with minimum offer size of Rs. 500 crore
Rs. 100 crore with minimum offer size of Rs. 50 crore

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The value of assets owned or proposed to be owned by a REIT for an initial offer shall not be less than Rs. 500 crore, and the minimum offer size shall not be less than Rs. 250 crore. Minimum 200 unit holders (other than sponsors/related parties) are required. **The Trap:** InvITs have the same thresholds (Rs. 500 crore assets, Rs. 250 crore offer size), which can cause confusion. SM REITs have different, smaller requirements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VI (Depository Operations) covers REIT/InvIT units in the demat context. Series V-A covers REITs as investment products. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** REIT and InvIT share the same asset/offer thresholds (Rs. 500 Cr / Rs. 250 Cr). Key difference: REIT invests in commercial real estate, InvIT in infrastructure. Both list mandatorily on exchanges.
